idea-plugin.name: LogPose
idea-plugin.description:
LogPose — read your Android network traffic without the logcat pain

LogPose gives you a tool window that captures each HTTP request/response as a single, structured transaction — no interleaved lines, no mismatched bodies, no manual JSON pretty-printing.

  • Master/detail view with collapsible JSON trees and a real JSON editor (folding) in Raw mode
  • One-click filter bar: URL search, Method & Status toggles, and a Hide-noise switch
  • Copy as cURL, copy section/transaction JSON, find within a body
  • First-class multipart upload (S3 / GCS) metadata instead of raw bytes
  • Adapts to your IDE theme (light & dark)

Requires a one-line OkHttp interceptor (logpose-android) in your app's debug/staging build, which emits one structured line per transaction. Works in IntelliJ IDEA and Android Studio.
idea-plugin.change-notes:
• Duplicate-call detection — repeated identical requests fired in a quick burst are flagged with a DUP ×N tag; overlapping non-idempotent calls (double-submits) are highlighted in red, with a warning in the overview and a "Dupes" filter to isolate them.
  • Raw JSON now colors keys vs. values (matching the tree), shows line numbers, and pre-collapses deeply-nested nodes on large payloads.
  • Response headers are hidden by default behind a "Headers" toggle (request headers stay shown) — no more CSP noise drowning the body.
  • Light-theme support (adapts to the IDE theme)
  • Raw JSON mode is now a real editor with code folding
  • One-click filter bar (method/status toggles, Hide-noise switch)
  • Find in request/response, server trace-id in the overview
  • Compass icon, helpful empty state
